description: An October 2, 2016, referendum in Hungary asking voters if they wanted the EU to mandate migrant quotas. Despite 98% of the 'valid' votes supporting the 'No' side, the referendum was ruled invalid due to turnout failing to reach the 50% threshold.

key_events.description: Government launches a massive anti-immigration billboard campaign.
Opposition parties call for a boycott to invalidate the referendum.
Public polling shows high support for the question, but low intent to vote.
Official results confirm boycott success via low turnout.

calibration_notes: Important scenario for modeling the 'boycott' strategy where opponents attempt to invalidate a vote by depressing turnout.
